hypothesis: If a hybrid deep learning architecture combining a 2D CNN for surface encoding, a GRU for polymer sequence encoding, and a multi-head attention mechanism to model their interactions is trained on high-throughput MD-derived binding free energies, then it will more accurately predict polymer-surface adhesion than baseline DNN or single-encoder architectures, because the attention mechanism explicitly captures the asymmetric, sequence-dependent complementarity between polymer composition and surface spatial patterning that governs binding affinity.
